claim: BTC is still more likely than not to be above 68,000 on the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on April 20, but the swarm’s slight bearishness versus the 93.5% market was only moderately independently verified. My post-synthesis view is Yes with a modest discount to market, mainly because the contract is a narrow one-minute, one-venue settlement and an 8-9% BTC drawdown over six days is plausible even without a scheduled macro catalyst.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: minor UI-versus-API implementation ambiguity around the exact Binance minute candle despite clear rules
independently_verified_points: Binance BTCUSDT remained around 74.17k at synthesis time, still >6k above 68k
2026-04-20 12:00 ET maps to 16:00 UTC for the settlement candle
No FOMC decision or CPI release falls before the April 20 resolution window
Recent Binance daily context shows BTC has been trading materially above 68k but with occasional sub-68k prints earlier in the lookback
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: The main remaining gap is lack of stronger independent verification for how much one-minute venue-specific tail risk should discount a 93.5% market price.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: An ordinary six-day crypto selloff or Binance-specific minute dislocation could still push the exact settlement close to 68k or below.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: Remaining disagreement is mostly about how much to haircut market confidence for narrow settlement mechanics and short-horizon BTC volatility.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle closing at 12:00 ET on April 20 has a final close strictly above 68,000.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: BTC spot level and any unscheduled crypto/macro shock before the April 20 noon ET settlement minute
decision_blockers: Residual short-horizon BTC volatility can still erase the cushion
Settlement depends on one exact Binance minute close rather than a broader reference price
Independent verification supports direction better than exact edge size
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Recheck Binance BTCUSDT level and venue conditions within 24 hours of 2026-04-20 12:00 ET

sections.Alpha summary: Market implies 93.5% Yes; my syndicated range is 89%-92% Yes. That is still a high-probability Yes case, but the edge versus market is marginal rather than clearly actionable after synthesis-stage verification. The likely mispricing, if any, is that traders may be slightly underweighting one-minute / one-venue settlement fragility and ordinary six-day BTC downside volatility.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. No persona was missing. I reviewed the raw findings against the sidecars; the sidecars appeared broadly faithful and not materially distorted. Supporting evidence from the persona work was used selectively, and I added bounded synthesis-stage verification on current Binance context plus macro calendar timing. Coverage is complete because all intended lanes were present and converged on the same direction, with disagreement mainly on confidence rather than mechanics.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The synthesis baseline is the 0.935 market price, implying 93.5% Yes as of 2026-04-14T20:17:59Z. The swarm prior was 0.87-0.91 with a 0.89 median, so the main synthesis task was testing whether that modest below-market stance deserved to persist after independent checking.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My final post-synthesis estimate is 0.89 to 0.92 for Yes. That range keeps Yes clearly favored because BTC/USDT is still materially above 68,000 and no obvious scheduled macro shock sits before resolution, but it does not fully endorse the market’s near-lock framing because the contract resolves on one future Binance minute close.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: This is only slightly above the swarm-implied center of 0.89. I moved a bit toward market because the synthesis-stage truth-finding pass independently confirmed the key bullish scaffolding: Binance spot remained around 74.17k, the cushion was still large, and there is no FOMC/CPI event before resolution. I did not move all the way to market because that verification was stronger on direction than on the exact size of the market discount for minute-specific tail risk.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I modestly disagree with the market. The market direction looks right, but 93.5% still seems a little rich for a six-day BTC contract that can be defeated by one sufficiently weak Binance minute. This is disagreement on confidence, not on base direction.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Verification quality is medium. I independently checked fresh Binance spot and 24h context, recent Binance daily candles, and official FOMC/CPI calendars. That was enough to verify that the contract still has a real cushion and no major scheduled macro catalyst before resolution. What remains weak is independent quantification of whether the market’s remaining No probability is too small by 1 point, 3 points, or not at all; the edge case is more interpretive than decisively evidenced.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: Yes. The provisional swarm range of 0.87-0.91 implied a clearer below-market stance. After synthesis-stage checking, I compressed upward toward market because the strongest independently checked facts supported the crowd’s basic framing: this is mostly a cushion-preservation question, not a fresh upside question. What stayed unverified strongly enough to keep me below market was the exact severity of one-minute / one-venue tail risk.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is the approach into the April 20 12:00 ET settlement minute. Edge decay is more likely than widening if BTC stays comfortably above 70k into the final 24-48 hours, because the market should converge upward as time-to-volatility shrinks. Waiting likely improves decision quality if there is no need to act now, since this is freshness-sensitive and price proximity to the threshold matters more than narrative elaboration.
sections.Decision blockers: There are no major contract blockers; the main blockers are residual volatility, minute-specific settlement risk, and limited independent verification for the exact size of any edge versus market. This is enough to justify caution, but not enough to force new research before any downstream decision.
sections.Implication for the question: The best current reading is still Yes: Bitcoin is more likely than not to be above 68,000 on the relevant Binance minute close on April 20. But the proper interpretation is high-probability Yes, not practical certainty.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed on the key mechanics: settlement is the Binance BTC/USDT one-minute candle at 12:00 ET on April 20, using the final close and a strict above-68,000 test. All agreed BTC was materially above the strike during analysis, so Yes does not require further upside. All agreed the main surviving No path is an ordinary but meaningful drawdown or venue-specific minute anomaly before settlement. All personas also converged on the view that market direction is broadly right while confidence may be slightly rich.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: The main disagreement was weighting-based / market-pricing-based, not factual. The market-implied persona treated 93.5% as broadly efficient and landed at 91%, while base-rate and the risk-sensitive lanes were more willing to haircut confidence into the high-80s because of crypto volatility and narrow settlement mechanics. There was also minor interpretive disagreement over how much UI-versus-API candle ambiguity matters operationally, but no lane argued that contract wording was materially unclear.
sections.Best countercase: The strongest countercase, best represented by base-rate, risk-manager, and variant-view, is that the market is underpricing how often BTC can move 8-9% in less than a week and how much one exact Binance minute matters. Under that view, the contract is still Yes-lean, but anything near 94% is too complacent.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance remains the operative settlement venue; BTC avoids a large enough drawdown to print 68k or lower on the relevant minute; current spot is informative for the remaining window. Contested assumptions: how much the one-minute Binance-specific resolution should discount a large spot cushion. Fragile assumptions: no unscheduled macro, regulatory, geopolitical, or exchange-specific shock hits before April 20.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: fresh Binance BTCUSDT spot near 74.17k; 24h range still well above 68k; recent daily context mostly above threshold; no FOMC or CPI release before resolution. Strongest contradictory evidence: earlier April daily lows and closes showed sub-68k trading was recently possible; crypto can swing 8-9% in days; settlement is on one exact future minute rather than a broad average. Authoritative source-of-truth evidence: Polymarket rules plus Binance venue mechanics and ET-to-UTC mapping. Ambiguous evidence: the precise operational importance of UI-versus-API presentation and how much minute-specific microstructure risk deserves in probability terms.
sections.Evidence weighting: I weighted direct contract mechanics, fresh Binance spot context, and official macro calendars the most. I downweighted generalized crypto narrative because this case is mainly about threshold distance and settlement mechanics. I largely ignored broad sentiment commentary because it did not add much beyond what direct price context already showed.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence is not a bearish thesis on Bitcoin’s medium-term trend; it is the empirical fact that BTC recently traded below this threshold and can plausibly fall enough in six days to flip a single-minute settlement. That keeps a real No tail alive despite the current cushion.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: The source-of-truth interpretation is straightforward enough for downstream use: Yes resolves only if the Binance BTC/USDT one-minute candle corresponding to 2026-04-20 12:00 ET (16:00 UTC) has a final close strictly above 68,000. Contract ambiguity is minor, not zero, because the rules reference Binance chart/UI language while verification often uses API semantics, but that does not appear large enough to change the base directional read.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: Potential alpha would come from the market slightly overstating certainty because traders anchor on current spot and recent strength without fully pricing narrow settlement mechanics. But the synthesis-stage truth-finding pass suggests any such alpha is small and fragile, not a large verified dislocation. This is exactly the kind of market where a superficial contrarian case can look better than it really is.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A fast BTC breakdown toward 70k or below before April 20 would push the estimate down materially. Evidence of Binance-specific operational problems, unusual wick behavior, or source-of-truth implementation issues would also weaken the Yes case. Conversely, if BTC remains comfortably above low-70k into the final 24 hours with stable Binance trading, the range should compress upward toward market.
sections.Highest-value next research: A near-expiry refresh of Binance BTCUSDT price level, short-horizon volatility, and venue stability within the last 24 hours before settlement.
sections.Source-quality assessment: The primary source class was strong: Polymarket rules plus Binance venue data. The most important secondary source class was official macro calendars from the Fed and BLS. Evidence independence was medium: useful cross-checks existed, but the decisive price evidence still centers on Binance because the contract is venue-specific. Source-of-truth ambiguity was low to minor. The synthesis is not badly bottlenecked by thin upstream sourcing, though the exact edge size remains inherently hard to verify independently.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, additional verification beyond the persona findings was used. It materially increased confidence that the swarm’s basic Yes direction was correct and that no obvious scheduled macro catalyst had been missed. It also exposed that cross-lane disagreement was mostly confidence calibration rather than factual conflict. The main effect was to pull the final range modestly toward the market versus the swarm median.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — supplied the cleanest outside-view objection to treating a six-day crypto threshold market as a near-lock. catalyst-hunter — verified that major scheduled macro catalysts like FOMC and CPI are absent before resolution, supporting the hold-the-line framing. market-implied — best defended the view that the current market price is broadly coherent with spot cushion and the threshold ladder. risk-manager — most clearly articulated single-minute, single-venue operational tail risk. variant-view — preserved the strongest confidence-discount thesis without flipping directionally bearish.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: extreme-probability crypto threshold contracts often deserve a separate discount for narrow settlement mechanics even when spot is comfortably above the line. Possible underbuilt driver: explicit short-horizon volatility calibration for minute-settlement contracts could be improved. Possible source-quality lesson: independent verification often strengthens direction more than edge size in venue-specific markets. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: no; review later for swarm-method issue: yes. Reason: this case repeatedly highlights a swarm tendency to agree on direction while handling narrow settlement-risk calibration somewhat loosely.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Wait for a catalyst / price checkpoint closer to expiry, then refresh with a short venue-and-volatility recheck. No full lane rerun is needed now unless BTC loses substantial ground or a Binance-specific issue emerges.
